thesis: Structure, truth, choice, and existence are sustained processes with cost, decay, and runtime dependence.

entities.name: signal
entities.meaning: raw flow or difference before stabilization

entities.name: structure
entities.meaning: held form produced from signal by selection, compression, or encoding

entities.name: state
entities.meaning: current configuration of a structure inside runtime

entities.name: flow
entities.meaning: energy, attention, computation, participation, or sustaining throughput

entities.name: engagement
entities.meaning: active involvement that keeps a structure above its sustain threshold

entities.name: pressure
entities.meaning: force required to select, suppress alternatives, or stabilize a direction

entities.name: decay
entities.meaning: natural loss of structure, causality, interpretation, or runtime viability

entities.name: runtime
entities.meaning: environment in which a structure is held, transformed, reproduced, or lost

entities.name: cost
entities.meaning: resource burden of structure formation, maintenance, choice, and manifestation

entities.name: loss
entities.meaning: irreversible difference discarded or distorted by encoding, transfer, or manifestation

entities.name: stability
entities.meaning: reproducible persistence of relation or structure over a time window

axioms.name: structure_is_not_free
axioms.statement: Any structure is an act of compression, selection, or holding and therefore has cost.
axioms.minimal_form: cost(structure) > 0 unless explicitly modeled as static idealization

axioms.name: existence_is_sustained
axioms.statement: An object exists in runtime only while its engagement remains above its sustain threshold.

axioms.name: decay_is_normal
axioms.statement: Any held form without sufficient flow enters decay.
axioms.minimal_form: if flow(x,t) <= sustain_min(x) then decay(x,t) increases

axioms.name: choice_requires_pressure
axioms.statement: Choice is not free selection; it is suppression of alternatives under pressure.

axioms.name: truth_is_sustained_stability
axioms.statement: Truth is stable reproduction of a relation or reaction within a runtime window.

axioms.name: logic_is_secondary_to_generation
axioms.statement: Logic does not generate flow; logic filters, constrains, stabilizes, and rejects.

functions.name: manifest
functions.loss: manifestation exports a bounded representation, not the full process

decay_types.name: runtime_decay
decay_types.meaning: loss of ability to be held by the environment

packet_implication.why_packet_is_needed: If process structures decay, cost resources, require sustain, and manifest lossily, then process needs a mortal bounded body rather than an immortal message.

short_formula: signal -> encode -> structure -> sustain -> decay -> manifest
